Job Tax Credits applied to payroll withholding liability essentially represent new cash flow for the company.

Carryforward years:
The research credit cannot reduce the corporation’s excise below the minimum excise and cannot reduce the excise over $25,000 by more than 75%. Credits which exceed these limitations can be carried over to future tax years and applied against the corporate excise.  There are two types of carryovers:  
 
Unlimited Carryover  A corporation may carry over for an unlimited period of time any portion of the credit which is disallowed under the 75% limitation. Credits disallowed under the 75% limitation equal 25% of the corporation’s excise which exceeds $25,000.  
 
15-Year Carryover  Any credits disallowed which are not given unlimited status, may be carried forward for fifteen years.

9/5 model math summary:
Top-line: If Yes, sum of years 1-3 benefits + years 4 onwards

Details:
- First 3 years, 2.875% credit on qualified research expenses (assumed 100% of R&D expenses in state P&L). 
- Year 4 onwards: three steps. 1) Prior 3-year avg. QRE. 2) Excess over 50% of the prior 3 year avg. QRE. 3) Credit of 5.75% * Step #2. 

Note
- Starting in tax year 2018, up to 10% of the tax credit can be refundable. The rest can be carried forward. For now, model assumes 100% fully nonrefundable
Carryforward years: Varies: Up to 10% of credit is refundable; rest is non-refundable and carry forward for 15 years
Eligible taxes to abate: WI income and franchise taxes; then must carry forward. Model currently treats 100% of tax credit as nonrefundable but able to carry forward
Notes:
- Did not include higher credit activities. 
- Note: WI requires tax credits to be claimed as income, regardless of whether credit is used in that year (more of an FYI)

9/5 model math summary:
Top-line: If Yes, sum #1-5 depending on individual benefit eligibility

Note: Assumed "No" which requires manual "Yes". WI designates EZ based on project (not by geography ahead of a project like in most EZ programs). If no state selected, assumed Non-distressed/Tier II designation.

Eligibility: 
1. Job creation tax credit for 12 years.
A-B) FTEs * average zone payroll (which is average salary; max $100K)
C) Subtract (A-B) minus threshold wages (if Tier 1 geography, 2080 hours * 150% of the federal minimum wage; if Tier 2, $30K)
D) Multiply (C. Avg. eligible wage amount) * FTEs. 
E) Multiply (D) by max 7% job creation credit. 

2. Job retention tax credit for 5 years.
- If Yes to retention, total payroll (FTEs * average wages) * 7% max credit for 5 years

3. Training tax credit. 
100% of qualifying training expenses * average training cost per employee of $1,481 from two studies (ADP and elearning) * FTEs for 1 year

4. Supply chain tax credit. Did not integrate. Benefit is up to 1% of the amount of qualified purchases made from WI vendors (I think  Foxconn deal had this arrangement)

5. Significant capital expenditure tax credit. 
If Yes, 10% credit on capex (technically, up to 10%) * qualified capex (land, buildings, and M&E) for 12 years
